2606.07130.title: Explicit Evidence Grounding via Structured Inline Citation Generation
2606.07130.paper_summary: As AI systems become more widely adopted, the demand for factual and faithful generation grows. Properly attributing information through citations becomes, therefore, crucial. This work introduces FullCite, a framework that, in contrast to most previous works, generates structured inline citations linking each claim to both its source document and supporting evidence. FullCite proposes three strategies to inline citation generation: prompt-based generation, constrained decoding over a citation grammar, and posthoc span alignment. Using three question answering benchmarks, namely, ASQA, BioASQ, and ExpertQA, we assess citation quality and faithfulness along three dimensions: document-level correctness, evidence span identification, and claim-citation faithfulness. Our evaluation shows that while LLMs are generally effective at identifying relevant documents, they struggle to identify the precise supporting spans within them. This gap suggests that achieving faithful attributed QA will require research to place greater emphasis on precise evidence span identification.
2606.07130.paper_summary_zh: 隨著人工智慧系統的廣泛採用，對於事實和忠實生成的需求也在增長。因此，通過引用正確地歸屬信息變得至關重要。這項工作介紹了FullCite，一個框架，與大多數先前的工作相比，它生成結構化的內嵌引用，將每個主張鏈接到其來源文件和支持證據。FullCite提出了三種內嵌引用生成策略：基於提示的生成、在引用語法上的約束解碼，以及事後跨度對齊。通過三個問答基準，即ASQA、BioASQ和ExpertQA，我們在三個維度上評估引用的質量和忠實性：文檔級正確性、證據跨度識別和主張-引用的忠實性。我們的評估顯示，雖然大型語言模型在識別相關文檔方面通常是有效的，但它們在識別其中精確的支持跨度方面卻存在困難。這一差距表明，實現忠實歸屬的問答需要研究更強調精確的證據跨度識別。
2606.07130.author: Anar Yeginbergen et.al.
2606.07130.authors: Anar Yeginbergen, Amelie Wührl, Anna Rogers, Rodrigo Agerri
